session.user:
# /erk:plan-implement
Implement a plan - either from GitHub, a markdown file, an existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`, or by saving the current plan first.
This is the primary implementation workflow - it orchestrates:
1. Setting up the impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` (from issue, file, existing folder, or fresh plan) 2. Executing the implementation 3. Running CI and submitting the PR
## Prerequisites
- Must be in a git repository managed by erk - GitHub CLI (`gh`) must be authenticated - One of: - A plan number, URL, or file path argument - An existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - A plan branch checked out (e.g., `plnd/...` or `P{number}-...`) - A plan in `~/.claude/plans/` (from plan mode)
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-implement # Use impl directory, detect from branch, or save current plan /erk:plan-implement 2521 # Fetch and implement PR #2521 /erk:plan-implement https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521 # URL form /erk:plan-implement ./my-plan.md # Implement from local markdown file ```
---
## CRITICAL: PR Operations
**NEVER** run direct GitHub CLI commands for PR operations during implementation:
- `gh pr edit` — destroys plan-header metadata block - `gh pr ready` — bypasses lifecycle tracking - `gh pr close` / `gh pr merge`
All PR operations are handled by `erk pr submit` in Step 13. It automatically:
- Generates the PR title and body - Preserves the plan-header metadata block - Marks draft PRs as ready for review - Updates lifecycle stage
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Set Up Implementation
Parse `` and run the consolidated setup command:
- **If numeric** (e.g., `2521`): `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/.../issues/2521`): Extract number, `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If path to file** (anything else non-empty): `erk exec setup-impl --file <path>` - **If empty**: `erk exec setup-impl` (auto-detects from `.erk/impl-context/`, branch, or fails)
```bash erk exec setup-impl [--issue <N> | --file <path>] ```
This single command handles:
- Fetching plan from GitHub (draft PR) - Setting up from a local markdown file - Auto-detecting from existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - Auto-detecting plan number from branch name (P{number}-... or PR lookup) - Creating/checking out the feature branch - Creating impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` with plan content - Running impl-init validation - Cleaning up `.erk/impl-context/` staging directory (git rm + commit + push)
Otherwise, generate a branch slug and set up from the specified issue:
1. Fetch the title: `gh pr view <ISSUE_ARG> --json title -q .title` 2. Generate a branch slug from the title: - 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc" 3. Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <ISSUE_ARG> --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
If `setup-impl` exits with code 1 and `error: "no_plan_found"`, fall back to saving the current plan:
Generate a branch slug from the plan title (the first `# ` heading in the plan file):
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`
Save the current plan to GitHub and capture the plan number:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="c4c4b1d4-50a5-437a-b071-d640516df130" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
Parse the JSON output to get `pr_number`, then:
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <pr_number> ```
The `setup-impl` output includes `related_docs` (skills and docs to load) and `has_plan_tracking` (whether GitHub issue tracking is active).
If this fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 2: Read Plan and Load Context
Read `plan.md` from the impl directory set up in Step 1 to understand:
- Overall goal and context - Context & Understanding sections (API quirks, architectural insights, pitfalls) - Implementation phases and dependencies - Success criteria
**Context Consumption**: Plans contain expensive discoveries. Ignoring `[CRITICAL:]` tags, "Related Context:" subsections, or "DO NOT" items causes repeated mistakes.
### Step 3: Load Related Documentation
If plan contains "Related Documentation" section, load listed skills via Skill tool and read listed docs.
### Step 4: Create TodoWrite Entries
Create todo entries for each phase from the plan.
### Step 5: Signal GitHub Started
```bash erk exec impl-signal started --session-id="c4c4b1d4-50a5-437a-b071-d640516df130" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This also deletes the Claude plan file (from `~/.claude/plans/`) since:
- The content has been saved to GitHub - The content has been snapshotted to `.erk/scratch/` - Keeping it could cause confusion if the user tries to re-save
### Step 6: Execute Each Phase Sequentially
For each phase:
1. **Mark phase as in_progress** (in TodoWrite) 2. **Read task requirements** carefully 3. **Implement code AND tests together**: - Load `dignified-python-313` skill for coding standards - Load `fake-driven-testing` skill for test patterns - Follow project AGENTS.md standards 4. **Mark phase as completed** (in TodoWrite) 5. **Report progress**: changes made, what's next
**Important:** `plan.md` is immutable - NEVER edit during implementation
### Step 7: Report Progress
After each phase: report changes made and what's next.
### Step 8: Final Verification
Confirm all tasks executed, success criteria met, note deviations, summarize changes.
### Step 9: Signal GitHub Ended
```bash erk exec impl-signal ended --session-id="c4c4b1d4-50a5-437a-b071-d640516df130" 2>/dev/null || true ```
### Step 10: Upload Session for Async Learn
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id="c4c4b1d4-50a5-437a-b071-d640516df130" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This reads plan reference from the impl directory, captures session info, and uploads for async learn processing.
### Step 11: Clean Up Impl Context
Remove the `.erk/impl-context/` directory from the repo:
```bash erk exec cleanup-impl-context ```
This removes `.erk/impl-context/`, stages the deletion, commits, and pushes — all idempotent.
### Step 12: Run CI Iteratively
1. If `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` exists: follow its instructions 2. Otherwise: check CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for CI commands
### Step 13: Submit PR
Push the branch and create or update the PR using the Graphite-aware submit pipeline:
```bash erk pr submit ```
This handles pushing commits, creating/updating the PR, generating the PR description, and enhancing with Graphite stack metadata when available.
After successful submission, signal lifecycle transition:
```bash erk exec impl-signal submitted 2>/dev/null || true ```
Then validate PR completion invariants:
```bash erk pr check --stage=impl ```
This validates PR submission readiness including implementation-specific checks (e.g., `.erk/impl-context/` must be cleaned up). If checks fail, display output and warn user.
### Step 14: Output Format
- **Start**: "Setting up implementation..." or "Fetching plan #X..." - **After setup**: "Implementation environment ready, reading plan..." - **Each phase**: "Phase X: [brief description]" with code changes - **End**: "Plan execution complete. [Summary]"
---
## Related Commands
- `/erk:plan-save` - Save plan only, don't implement (for defer-to-later workflow) - `/erk:replan` - Re-plan an existing plan with current codebase state

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Auto-detect and set up implementation from branch

session.assistant: Implementation environment ready. Let me read the plan.
session.tool_result:
1→# Inline Roadmap JSON into objective-save-to-issue 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→The objective creation pipeline is fragile because it relies on Claude to: (1) construct JSON, (2) pipe it to `objective-render-roadmap`, (3) capture the output, (4) paste it into a markdown file. Claude can skip steps 2-3 and hand-write markdown tables, resulting in objectives with no YAML source of truth. This happened with #9394. 6→ 7→**Principle:** YAML is the source of truth for the roadmap dependency graph. Markdown tables are a rendering of it. We never parse markdown tables. 8→ 9→**Fix:** `objective-save-to-issue` accepts `--roadmap-json <path>` and handles YAML creation + markdown rendering internally. Delete `objective-render-roadmap`. 10→ 11→## Changes 12→ 13→### 1. Add `PhaseMetadata` dataclass and `roadmap_nodes_from_json()` to roadmap.py 14→ 15→**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py` 16→ 17→Add `PhaseMetadata` frozen dataclass (captures phase-level info from JSON that isn't stored in YAML: `number`, `name`, `description`, `pr_count`, `test`). 18→ 19→Add `roadmap_nodes_from_json(data: dict) -> tuple[list[RoadmapNode], list[PhaseMetadata], str | None]`: 20→- Absorbs validation logic from `_validate_input()` in `objective_render_roadmap.py` 21→- Absorbs RoadmapNode construction + slug generation via `slugify_node_description()` 22→- Returns `(nodes, phase_metadata, error_message)` 23→ 24→### 2. Add `render_initial_roadmap_section()` to roadmap.py 25→ 26→**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py` 27→ 28→Add `render_initial_roadmap_section(nodes: list[RoadmapNode], phase_metadata: list[PhaseMetadata]) -> str`: 29→- Renders phase headers using JSON's `pr_count` field (e.g., "### Phase 1: Steelthread (1 PR)") 30→- Renders markdown tables using `escape_md_table_cell()` (reuse existing pattern from `render_roadmap_tables`)
... [88 lines omitted] ...
70→3. On error, output JSON error and exit 1 91→- Remove "Do NOT manually write roadmap tables" warning (no longer relevant) 102→- `--roadmap-json` with invalid JSON → error output
session.assistant: Plan #9402: Inline Roadmap JSON into objective-save-to-issue. Let me load the relevant context and source files.

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Phase 1: Add PhaseMetadata and roadmap_nodes_from_json() to roadmap.py
Add PhaseMetadata frozen dataclass and roadmap_nodes_from_json(data) function to roadmap.py

session.tool_result: Task #1 created successfully: Phase 1: Add PhaseMetadata and roadmap_nodes_from_json() to roadmap.py
Task #2 created successfully: Phase 2: Add render_initial_roadmap_section() to roadmap.py

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Add roadmap_nodes and phase_metadata parameters to create_objective_issue()

session.tool_result: Task #3 created successfully: Phase 3: Modify create_objective_issue() signature

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Add Click option --roadmap-json to the exec script

session.tool_result: Task #4 created successfully: Phase 4: Add --roadmap-json to objective-save-to-issue

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Delete the script, tests, remove from exec group, remove from reference docs

session.tool_result: Task #5 created successfully: Phase 5: Delete objective-render-roadmap

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Update the skill to use --roadmap-json instead of piping through objective-render-roadmap

session.tool_result: Task #6 created successfully: Phase 6: Update /erk:objective-create skill

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Write tests for roadmap_nodes_from_json, render_initial_roadmap_section, and objective-save-to-issue --roadmap-json

session.tool_result: Task #7 created successfully: Phase 7: Tests

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Update objective-create-workflow.md and remove references to objective-render-roadmap

session.tool_result: Task #8 created successfully: Phase 8: Update docs
session.assistant: Now let me read all the source files I need to understand before writing code.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Shared parser, serializer, and data types for objective roadmap operations. 2→ 3→This module provides: 4→- Data types: RoadmapNodeStatus, RoadmapNode, RoadmapPhase 5→- Parsing: parse_roadmap() (v2 frontmatter only) 6→- Frontmatter: validate, parse, group, update 7→- Utilities: compute_summary(), find_next_node(), serialize_phases() 8→ 9→Previously split across objective_roadmap_shared.py and 10→objective_roadmap_frontmatter.py in the erk package. Consolidated 11→here to eliminate the circular dependency between erk_shared and erk. 12→""" 13→ 14→import re 15→from collections.abc import Mapping 16→from dataclasses import dataclass, replace 17→from typing import Literal, cast 18→ 19→import yaml 20→ 21→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 22→ extract_raw_metadata_blocks, 23→ parse_metadata_block_body, 24→) 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys 26→ 27→RoadmapNodeStatus = Literal["pending", "planning", "done", "in_progress", "blocked", "skipped"] 28→ 29→ 30→@dataclass(frozen=True)
... [843 lines omitted] ...
67→ Tuple of (steps, errors). If validation succeeds, 68→ errors is empty. If validation fails, steps is None. 70→ errors: list[str] = [] 75→ errors.append("Missing required field: schema_version") 76→ return None, errors 79→ errors.append(f"Unsupported schema_version: {schema_version}") 80→ return None, errors 88→ errors.append("Missing required field: nodes (or steps for v2)") 89→ return None, errors 91→ errors.append("Field 'steps' must be a list") 92→ return None, errors 98→ errors.append(f"Step {i} is not a mapping") 99→ return None, errors 106→ errors.append(f"Step {i} missing required field: {field}") 107→ return None, errors 119→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'id' must be a string") 120→ return None, errors 122→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'description' must be a string") 123→ return None, errors 125→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'status' must be a string") 126→ return None, errors 129→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'status' must be one of: {valid_statuses}") 130→ return None, errors 132→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'pr' must be a string or null") 133→ return None, errors 139→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'depends_on' must be a list or null") 140→ return None, errors 143→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'depends_on' item {j} must be a string") 144→ return None, errors 149→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'slug' must be a string or null") 150→ return None, errors 154→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'comment' must be a string or null") 155→ return None, errors 169→ return steps, errors 189→ except ValueError: 191→ steps, _errors = validate_roadmap_frontmatter(data) 198→ "<!-- WARNING: Machine-generated. Manual edits may break erk tooling. -->\n" 520→ ``(phases, validation_errors)`` on success, or ``None`` when the 539→ steps, errors = validate_roadmap_frontmatter(data) 545→ return (phases, errors) 548→_LEGACY_FORMAT_ERROR = ( 559→ (phases, validation_errors) 577→ # Block exists but failed to parse — legacy/broken format 578→ return ([], [_LEGACY_FORMAT_ERROR])

1→"""Render a complete roadmap section from structured JSON input. 2→ 3→Takes structured JSON from stdin describing phases/steps and outputs 4→a complete ## Roadmap markdown section with phase headers, tables, 5→test sections, and a machine-readable metadata block. 6→ 7→Usage: 8→ echo '<json>' | erk exec objective-render-roadmap 9→ 10→Input JSON: 11→ { 12→ "phases": [ 13→ { 14→ "name": "Steelthread", 15→ "description": "Minimal vertical slice.", 16→ "pr_count": "1 PR", 17→ "steps": [ 18→ {"id": "1.1", "description": "Minimal infrastructure"}, 19→ {"id": "1.2", "description": "Wire into one path"} 20→ ], 21→ "test": "End-to-end acceptance test" 22→ } 23→ ] 24→ } 25→ 26→Output: 27→ Complete ## Roadmap section including: 28→ - Phase headers (### Phase 1: Name (1 PR)) 29→ - Phase descriptions 30→ - 4-column markdown tables (Step | Description | Status | PR)
... [207 lines omitted] ...
59→ Tuple of (phases_list, error_message). If valid, error is None. 218→ json.dumps({"success": False, "error": "No input provided on stdin"}), 224→ except json.JSONDecodeError as e: 226→ json.dumps({"success": False, "error": f"Invalid JSON: {e}"}), 230→ phases, error = _validate_input(data) 231→ if error is not None: 232→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": error}))

1→"""GitHub issue creation for objectives and label management. 2→ 3→This module contains: 4→- create_objective_issue(): Create objective issues with roadmap metadata 5→- Label management utilities (definitions, ensuring labels exist) 6→- CreateObjectiveIssueResult: Result type shared by objective issue creation 7→ 8→Plan creation now uses create_plan_draft_pr() from pr_store.create_plan_draft_pr. 9→""" 10→ 11→from dataclasses import dataclass 12→from datetime import UTC 13→from pathlib import Path 14→ 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 17→ create_objective_header_block, 18→ extract_raw_metadata_blocks, 19→ format_objective_content_comment, 20→ render_metadata_block, 21→ update_objective_header_comment_id, 22→) 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.roadmap import ( 24→ parse_roadmap_frontmatter, 25→ render_objective_roadmap_block, 26→ render_roadmap_block_inner, 27→ rerender_comment_roadmap, 28→) 29→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys 30→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import BodyText
... [338 lines omitted] ...
59→ pr_number: PR number if created (may be set even on failure if 60→ partial success - issue created but comment failed) 63→ error: Error message if failed, None if success 70→ error: str | None 139→ Does NOT raise exceptions. All errors returned in result. 149→ error="Could not get GitHub username (gh CLI not authenticated?)", 165→ error=slug_result.message, 178→ label_errors = _ensure_labels_exist(github_issues, repo_root, labels) 179→ if label_errors: 185→ error=label_errors, 211→ except RuntimeError as e: 217→ error=f"Failed to create GitHub issue: {e}", 224→ except RuntimeError as e: 225→ # Partial success - issue created but comment failed 231→ error=f"Issue #{result.number} created but failed to add objective comment: {e}", 252→ error=None, 269→ Error message if failed, None if success 302→ except RuntimeError as e: 303→ return f"Failed to ensure labels exist: {e}"

1→"""Save plan as objective GitHub issue. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec objective-save-to-issue [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→This command extracts a plan and creates a GitHub issue with: 7→- erk-objective label only (NOT erk-pr - objectives are not plans) 8→- No title suffix 9→- Plan content directly in body (no metadata block) 10→- No commands section 11→ 12→Options: 13→ --session-id ID: Session ID for scoped plan lookup 14→ --format: json (default) or display 15→ --validate: Run objective validation after creation 16→ 17→Exit Codes: 18→ 0: Success - objective issue created 19→ 1: Error - no plan found, gh failure, etc. 20→""" 21→ 22→import json 23→from pathlib import Path 24→from typing import Any, cast 25→ 26→import click 27→ 28→from erk.cli.commands.objective.check.validation import ( 29→ ObjectiveValidationError, 30→ ObjectiveValidationSuccess,
... [240 lines omitted] ...
177→ click.echo("Error: No PR found in ~/.claude/plans/", err=True) 183→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": "No PR found in ~/.claude/plans/"})) 199→ click.echo(f"Error: {result.error}", err=True) 201→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": result.error})) 206→ raise RuntimeError("Unexpected: pr_number is None after success") 228→ if isinstance(validation_result, ObjectiveValidationError): 231→ "error": validation_result.error, 241→ "errors": validation_result.validation_errors, 252→ click.echo("Validation: FAILED")

1→--- 2→description: Create a structured objective through guided conversation 3→--- 4→ 5→# /erk:objective-create 6→ 7→Create a new objective through an interactive, guided process. You describe what you want to accomplish, and Claude proposes a structured objective for your approval. 8→ 9→## Usage 10→ 11→```bash 12→/erk:objective-create 13→``` 14→ 15→--- 16→ 17→## Agent Instructions 18→ 19→### Step 1: Prompt for Description 20→ 21→Ask the user to describe what they want to accomplish: 22→ 23→``` 24→What do you want to accomplish? Describe it however makes sense to you - goals, 25→constraints, design decisions you've already made, context about the codebase, etc. 26→ 27→I'll structure it into a formal objective for your review. 28→``` 29→ 30→Wait for the user's response. They may provide:
... [329 lines omitted] ...
351→## Error Cases 356→| Not authenticated | Report GitHub auth error | 357→| Issue creation fails | Report API error, offer to retry | 358→| Plan file write fails | Report error with path |

1→--- 2→title: Objectives Tripwires 3→read_when: 4→ - "working on objectives code" 5→--- 6→ 7→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 8→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 9→<!-- Generated from objectives/*.md frontmatter --> 10→ 11→# Objectives Tripwires 12→ 13→Rules triggered by matching actions in code. 14→ 15→**accessing node_id on a RoadmapNode** → Read [Roadmap Shared Parser Architecture](roadmap-parser-api.md) first. The field is named 'id', not 'node_id'. This is a common mistake — check the actual dataclass definition. 16→ 17→**accessing phase names from graph operations without calling enrich_phase_names()** → Read [Phase Name Enrichment](phase-name-enrichment.md) first. Phase names come from markdown headers, not from the parser. After graph_from_phases(), call enrich_phase_names(graph, issue_body) to populate phase names. Without enrichment, phase.name is None. 18→ 19→**adding a new exec command without registering in the exec group** → Read [Objective Exec Command Consolidation](exec-command-consolidation.md) first. New exec scripts must be registered in the Click exec command group and follow the existing pattern: Click command with typed options, LBYL validation, structured result TypedDict. 20→ 21→**adding a new roadmap mutation site without updating this document** → Read [Objective Lifecycle](objective-lifecycle.md) first. All roadmap mutation sites must be documented in objective-lifecycle.md 22→ 23→**adding a new validation check** → Read [Roadmap Validation Architecture](roadmap-validation.md) first. Structural checks go in parse_roadmap() and return warnings alongside data. Semantic checks go in validate_objective() and produce pass/fail results. Don't mix levels. 24→ 25→**adding a required parameter to objective-apply-landed-update without fallback discovery** → Read [Objective Exec Command Consolidation](exec-command-consolidation.md) first. The script auto-fills missing parameters from git/plan state. New parameters should follow the same pattern: explicit flag first, then auto-discovery fallback. 26→ 27→**adding columns to the roadmap table format** → Read [Roadmap Format Versioning](roadmap-format-versioning.md) first. Read this doc to understand the header-based detection and rendering strategy before adding columns. 28→ 29→**adding structural validation to check_cmd.py** → Read [Objective Check Command — Semantic Validation](objective-roadmap-check.md) first. Structural validation (phase headers, table format) belongs in roadmap.py (packages/erk-shared). check_cmd.py handles semantic validation only. 30→
... [78 lines omitted] ...
73→**raising exceptions from validate_objective()** → Read [Objective Check Command — Semantic Validation](objective-roadmap-check.md) first. validate_objective() returns discriminated unions, never raises. Only CLI presentation functions (\_output_json, \_output_human) raise SystemExit. 101→**using parse_roadmap() when strict v2 validation is needed** → Read [Roadmap Shared Parser Architecture](roadmap-parser-api.md) first. Use parse_v2_roadmap() for commands that should reject legacy format. parse_roadmap() returns a legacy error string; parse_v2_roadmap() returns None for non-v2 content.
session.assistant: Now let me read additional files I'll need.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Static exec group for erk scripts. 2→ 3→This module provides the `erk exec` command group with all scripts 4→statically registered. 5→""" 6→ 7→import click 8→ 9→# Import and register all scripts 10→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.add_objective_node import add_objective_node 11→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.add_pr_label import add_pr_label 12→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.add_pr_labels import add_pr_labels_batch 13→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.add_pr_labels_cmd import add_pr_labels 14→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.add_remote_execution_note import ( 15→ add_remote_execution_note, 16→) 17→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.capture_session_info import ( 18→ capture_session_info, 19→) 20→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.ci_fetch_summaries import ci_fetch_summaries 21→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.ci_generate_summaries import ci_generate_summaries 22→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.ci_update_pr_body import ci_update_pr_body 23→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.ci_verify_autofix import ci_verify_autofix 24→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.classify_pr_feedback import classify_pr_feedback 25→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.cleanup_impl_context import cleanup_impl_context 26→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.close_pr import ( 27→ close_pr, 28→) 29→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.close_prs import close_prs 30→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.cmux_checkout_workspace import cmux_open_pr
... [263 lines omitted] ...
167→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.summarize_impl_failure import ( 168→ summarize_impl_failure, 276→exec_group.add_command(summarize_impl_failure, name="summarize-impl-failure")

1→"""Unit tests for objective-render-roadmap command.""" 2→ 3→import json 4→import re 5→ 6→from click.testing import CliRunner 7→ 8→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.objective_render_roadmap import ( 9→ _render_roadmap, 10→ _validate_input, 11→ objective_render_roadmap, 12→) 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.roadmap import parse_roadmap_frontmatter 14→ 15→# --- Validation tests --- 16→ 17→ 18→def test_validate_input_valid_minimal() -> None: 19→ """Validate accepts minimal valid input.""" 20→ data = { 21→ "phases": [ 22→ { 23→ "name": "Steelthread", 24→ "steps": [ 25→ {"id": "1.1", "description": "First step"}, 26→ ], 27→ }, 28→ ], 29→ } 30→ phases, error = _validate_input(data)

31→ assert error is None 60→ phases, error = _validate_input(data) 61→ assert error is None 67→ _, error = _validate_input("not a dict") 68→ assert error == "Input must be a JSON object" 73→ _, error = _validate_input({}) 74→ assert error == "Missing required field: phases" 79→ _, error = _validate_input({"phases": "not a list"}) 80→ assert error == "Field 'phases' must be a list" 85→ _, error = _validate_input({"phases": []}) 86→ assert error == "Field 'phases' must not be empty" 92→ _, error = _validate_input(data) 93→ assert "missing required field: name" in error 99→ _, error = _validate_input(data) 100→ assert "missing required field: steps" in error 106→ _, error = _validate_input(data) 107→ assert "must not be empty" in error 113→ _, error = _validate_input(data) 114→ assert "missing required field: id" in error 120→ _, error = _validate_input(data) 121→ assert "missing required field: description" in error 254→ {"id": "1.1", "description": "SuccessType | ErrorType"}, 259→ assert r"SuccessType \| ErrorType" in result 295→ """CLI exits with error for empty input.""" 302→ assert "No input" in output["error"] 306→ """CLI exits with error for invalid JSON.""" 313→ assert "Invalid JSON" in output["error"] 317→ """CLI exits with error for valid JSON but invalid structure.""" 327→ assert "must be a list" in output["error"] 345→ phases, error = _validate_input(data) 346→ assert error is None 362→ _, error = _validate_input(data) 363→ assert error is not None 364→ assert "depends_on" in error 365→ assert "must be a list" in error 380→ _, error = _validate_input(data) 381→ assert error is not None 382→ assert "depends_on" in error 383→ assert "must be a string" in error

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- 3→ Regenerate: erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs 4→ CI check: erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs --check (included in make fast-ci) 5→ 6→ Source of truth: Live Click command tree in src/erk/cli/commands/exec/group.py 7→ Generator code: packages/erk-dev/src/erk_dev/exec_reference/generate.py 8→ Generator docs: docs/learned/cli/auto-generated-reference-docs.md 9→ 10→ Regenerate after: adding/modifying/removing exec commands or changing help text 11→--> 12→ 13→# erk exec Commands Reference 14→ 15→Quick reference for all `erk exec` subcommands. 16→ 17→## Summary 18→ 19→| Command | Description | 20→| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 21→| `add-objective-node` | Add a new node to an objective's roadmap. | 22→| `add-pr-label` | Add a label to a PR via the appropriate backend. | 23→| `add-pr-labels` | Add labels to a PR with automatic retry on transient failures. | 24→| `add-pr-labels-batch` | Batch add labels to multiple PRs from JSON stdin. | 25→| `add-remote-execution-note` | Add remote execution tracking note to PR body. | 26→| `capture-session-info` | Capture Claude Code session info for CI workflows. | 27→| `ci-fetch-summaries` | Fetch CI failure summaries for a PR. | 28→| `ci-generate-summaries` | Generate CI failure summaries using Haiku. | 29→| `ci-update-pr-body` | Update PR body with AI-generated summary and footer. | 30→| `ci-verify-autofix` | Run full CI verification after autofix push. |

77-| `objective-plan-setup` | Fetch, validate, and set up context for objective planning. | 78-| `objective-post-action-comment` | Post a formatted action comment to an objective issue. | 79:| `objective-render-roadmap` | Render a complete roadmap section from JSON input on stdin. | 80-| `objective-save-to-issue` | Save plan as objective GitHub issue. | 81-| `objective-update-after-land` | Update objective after landing a PR. | 82-| `plan-save` | Save plan as a planned PR. | 83-| `plan-update` | Update an existing plan's content. | 84-| `post-or-update-pr-summary` | Post or update a PR summary comment. | 85-| `post-pr-inline-comment` | Post an inline review comment on a PR. | 86-| `post-workflow-started-comment` | Post a workflow started comment to a GitHub issue. | 87-| `pr-sync-commit` | Sync PR title and body from the latest git commit. | 88-| `pre-tool-use-hook` | PreToolUse hook for dignified-python reminders on .py file edits. | 89-| `preprocess-session` | Preprocess session log JSONL to compressed XML format. | 90-| `push-and-create-pr` | Push branch and create/find PR, outputting JSON. | 91-| `push-session` | Preprocess and push a session to the planned-pr-context branch with accumulation. | 92-| `quick-submit` | Quick commit all changes and submit. | 93-| `rebase-with-conflict-resolution` | Rebase onto target branch and resolve conflicts with Claude. | 94-| `register-one-shot-pr` | Register a one-shot PR with issue metadata and comment. | 95-| `reopen-contested-threads` | Detect and reopen contested resolved PR review threads. | 96-| `reply-to-discussion-comment` | Reply to a PR discussion comment with quote and action summary. | 97-| `resolve-objective-ref` | Resolve an objective reference to an objective number. | 98-| `resolve-review-thread` | Resolve a PR review thread. | 99-| `resolve-review-threads` | Resolve multiple PR review threads from JSON stdin. | 100-| `run-review` | Run a code review using Claude. | 101-| `session-id-injector-hook` | Inject session ID into conversation context when relevant. | 102-| `set-local-review-marker` | Set local review marker on PR to skip CI reviews. | 103-| `set-pr-description` | Update PR title and body with agent-provided values. | 104-| `setup-impl` | Consolidated implementation setup. | 105-| `setup-impl-from-pr` | Set up .erk/impl-context/ folder from GitHub PR in current worktree. | 106-| `store-tripwire-candidates` | Store tripwire candidates as a metadata comment on a plan. |
